name: Clocktower
unlockCondition: Government project only, not privately constructable
calculationsAnalysis: The Clocktower represents specialized public infrastructure with technological significance. The construction cost of 20,000,000 ducats reflects the complex mechanical systems and architectural integration, while generating no direct income reflects its public service rather than commercial purpose. The maintenance cost of 75,000 ducats creates a substantial ongoing expense justified by its practical and symbolic importance. The employment capacity of 5 represents the specialized technicians needed to maintain the complex mechanism. The construction time of 40 days (3456000000ms) reflects the precision engineering project. This unique building serves as both practical timekeeper and symbol of Venetian technological prowess with appropriate restrictions preventing private construction, reflecting the historical reality of this singular structure that represented the Republic's orderly governance.
shortDescription: Public timepiece marking hours with automatons and astronomical indicators.
fullDescription: Regulating Venetian life through public timekeeping, the Clock Tower (Torre dell'Orologio) combines practical function with astronomical artistry. Featuring an ornate blue and gold clockface, zodiac indicators, automated figures striking the hours, and a bell at the top, this sophisticated mechanism represented cutting-edge Renaissance technology. Its prominent position overlooking St. Mark's Square symbolized Venice's orderly governance and scientific advancement.
flavorText: How better to demonstrate the Republic's mastery over commerce than to measure and display time itself—for in Venice, even the hours are counted, regulated, and transformed into the rhythm of prosperity.

locationRequirements.districtRestrictions: San Marco district, entrance to Mercerie from piazza
locationRequirements.adjacentRequirements: Visibility from main square, prominent position
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Creates public time reference, architectural focal point

constructionPhase3DPrompt: Renaissance Venetian Clocktower under construction, specialized public timepiece, tower structure rising from arch over street, precision clockwork mechanism being assembled by master horologists, astronomical dial installation with zodiac symbols, automated striking figures being installed on platform, golden detailing and blue enamel work, bell housing at top taking shape, archway underneath maintaining street access during construction, specialized craftsmen focusing on technical precision and artistic detail simultaneously, clockface being calibrated, isometric game perspective, stylized but detailed, focus on creating public chronometer that demonstrates Venetian technical skill and artistic refinement
completedBuilding3DPrompt: Completed Renaissance Venetian Clocktower, impressive public timepiece featuring ornate tower rising from arch spanning street entrance to Mercerie, large blue and gold clockface with astronomical indicators showing zodiac signs and lunar phases, famous Moors statues on top platform striking bell to mark hours, winged lion of St. Mark displayed prominently, intricate mechanical elements including procession of Wise Men on certain festivals, precision clockwork mechanism maintained by specialist technicians, archway below allowing pedestrian traffic between Piazza San Marco and shopping streets, historically accurate but slightly stylized for game aesthetic, isometric game perspective, sense of technological achievement combined with public practicality, perfect expression of Venetian appreciation for both art and science, citizens checking time as they pass, tourists admiring mechanical marvel, atmosphere of orderly civic life regulated by mechanical precision

soundDesign.ambientSound: Clockwork mechanisms, gentle ticking, pedestrians below
soundDesign.activitySounds: Bell striking hours, automated figures moving, crowd reactions
